concept: THE CLAIM ON THE TUNDRA — a gigawatt AI data center is proposed on a square mile of North Slope state land near Deadhorse. We shoot it as a blueprint trying to become real on ground that is still empty: a ghost wireframe campus assembles over the tundra, its own numbers refuse to settle, and the only thing at human scale is a flood of public comment. The honest limit is drawn, not stated: nothing is built, the figures span six times over, no customers or chips are confirmed, and the decision is still Alaska's to make by July 17th.

fingerprint.palette: silver-white overcast sky over sage and ochre tundra, gunmetal turbine steel, electric blueprint cyan wireframe, sodium flare orange, a single signal-red deadline flag
fingerprint.metaphor: the AI buildout drawn onto real Alaska land as a wireframe blueprint that is not built yet, its numbers leaping and refusing to settle, while a paper flood of public comment is the one thing at human scale that can hold it to account

palette: silver-white overcast sky over sage and ochre tundra, gunmetal turbine steel, electric blueprint cyan wireframe, sodium flare orange, a single signal-red deadline flag

light.sun: no sun disk; flat high overcast North Slope daylight, near shadowless, a cold silver sky the color of wet slate
light.mood: the light refuses to dramatize or glamorize the project; it is honest, plain, institutional daylight that lets the facts and the scale stand on their own

hook.frame1: flat grey North Slope tundra under a silver overcast sky, one lone survey stake with an orange flag in the foreground, the faint cyan ghost of a vast campus wireframe barely readable on the horizon, headline burned in lower-centered
hook.headline: The AI boom wants Alaska
hook.loopback: Shot 5 returns to this exact tundra and survey stake, the campus still only a ghost wireframe, now stamped with the July 17th deadline, closing on the unbuilt land

shots.centered: the lone survey stake with its orange flag, dead center, as the camera rises to reveal the vast empty tundra and the parcel outline drawing itself onto the land
shots.means: establish the real, empty, human-owned land BEFORE anything is built on it

hero.name: the survey stake on the tundra
hero.detail_note: weathered wood stake, bent orange flagging tape catching wind, a small brass cap, the ground shadow pooling in shallow tundra polygons; the stake is the only man-made thing for miles

shots.centered: a worm's-eye view from the tundra floor looking up as four wireframe gas-turbine stacks rise and assemble overhead against the flat silver sky, one sodium flare igniting at the crown
shots.means: we drop off the drone to human ground level; the proposal's scale looms up over us as it builds, still only edges, not a building

hero.name: the turbine stacks rising overhead
hero.detail_note: cyan blueprint edges of four gas-turbine stacks towering up from a ghosted gravel pad, cross-braced frames assembling rung by rung, an access gantry threading in, a single sodium flare lighting at the crown; deliberately unbuilt, edges only

shots.centered: a single cost bar at frame center that leaps and cannot settle between a five hundred million dollar tick and an over ten billion dollar tick, the gigawatt scale line beside it
shots.means: the honest caveat, drawn: the numbers refuse to sit still, so the certainty the project projects is not real

hero.name: the number that will not settle
hero.detail_note: a blueprint-cyan bar jittering on a gridline, a low tick labeled about five hundred million, a high ghost tick over ten billion, a separate one-gigawatt bar measured against a faint urban-Alaska baseline; blueprint register, not a brutalist dashboard

shots.centered: a towering drift of public-comment slips piling on the left against a tiny stack of a dozen on the right, the divide down frame center
shots.means: what is actually real and at human scale: the public wrote in, overwhelmingly against, and that record is the check on the machine

hero.name: the public comment, five hundred against a dozen
hero.detail_note: hundreds of pale paper slips drifting and stacking like snow into a wall, a lone short stack of a dozen opposite, individual slips tumbling in the wind, a hard center seam dividing the two counts

shots.centered: the same tundra and survey stake from Shot 1, the campus still only a faint ghost wireframe, a warning-amber July 17th deadline stamp settling at frame center
shots.means: loop back to the unbuilt land; the decision, and the deadline, still belong to Alaska

hero.name: the land, and the deadline
hero.detail_note: the returning tundra and orange-flagged stake, the ghost campus dimmer than before, an amber deadline stamp reading July 17th, the wordmark resolving; the land is what is real, the campus still is not

beats.vo: This week, Alaska is asked to power the A I boom.
beats.shows: tundra and lone stake; headline burns in; ghost campus flickers on the horizon

choreo.primary: camera begins its slow rise off the stake
choreo.ambient: low tundra wind moving the flag tape

beats.vo: A company wants a square mile of North Slope tundra,
beats.shows: the parcel outline draws itself onto the map of the land
beats.means: a claim is drawn on real ground

choreo.primary: parcel boundary lines walk across the tundra
choreo.reaction: corner survey pins drop and lock

beats.vo: twenty five miles south of Deadhorse, on state land.
beats.shows: a location pin sets; STATE LAND label settles

choreo.reaction: distance scale ticks out to Deadhorse

beats.vo: To run A I, it builds here,
beats.shows: the camera drops to the tundra floor; wireframe turbine frames begin to rise overhead
beats.means: the proposal builds onto the empty land, seen from the ground

choreo.primary: camera drops from the aerial to ground level

beats.vo: burning North Slope gas for a gigawatt of power.
beats.shows: turbine stacks snap in; one sodium flare lights
beats.means: the power is enormous and gas-fired

beats.vo: Thirty percent above urban Alaska's peak.
beats.shows: the gigawatt bar overshoots urban Alaska's whole peak; a plus thirty percent tick prints above the baseline
beats.means: scale made legible against something real

choreo.primary: gigawatt bar overshoots the urban baseline

beats.vo: But nothing is built here yet.
beats.shows: the whole campus drops to dashed ghost lines
beats.means: hard turn to the honest caveat

choreo.primary: solid edges fall to dashed wireframe

beats.vo: A proposal, and the numbers won't settle.
beats.shows: camera racks to a single cost bar in the void

choreo.primary: rack focus from campus to the cost bar

beats.vo: Early site work, five hundred million dollars.
beats.shows: the bar sits at the low tick, labeled

choreo.primary: bar settles on the low tick

beats.vo: The whole build, over ten billion.
beats.shows: the bar leaps to the high ghost tick and cannot hold
a CUSTOMERS tray and a CHIPS tray sit open, conspicuously empty and unsigned; a paper edge just begins to wipe in from frame right

choreo.primary: two labeled trays hold, empty and unsigned
choreo.reaction: paper edge wipes from frame right

beats.vo: What's real is the public comment.
beats.shows: the paper flood pours and stacks on the left

choreo.primary: hundreds of slips drift and pile into a wall

beats.vo: Over five hundred against, under a dozen for.
beats.shows: the two counts resolve across the center seam

beats.vo: The company called them form letters.
beats.shows: a FORM LETTER? stamp presses down on the tall five hundred stack but cannot grey it or move it
beats.means: the company pushes back on the record, and fails to erase it

choreo.primary: a dismissive stamp presses down on the tall stack

beats.shows: match-cut back to the tundra and stake; ghost campus faint
beats.means: loop back to the unbuilt land

choreo.primary: camera settles into a locked drift on the stake

beats.vo: The comment window closes July seventeenth.
beats.shows: a signal-red July 17th deadline stamp lands on the land as one heavy stamp
beats.means: the deadline is live and near

choreo.primary: the deadline stamp lands as one heavy stamp
choreo.reaction: stake flag lifts in a gust

beats.vo: It cannot grant itself the land. That call still belongs to Alaska.
beats.shows: wordmark resolves over the land; campus stays a ghost
beats.means: the button: the decision is Alaska's

build_steps: low tundra wind and a sub-bass drone under the empty land
a cold mechanical pulse as the campus assembles and the flare lights
unstable number ticks as the cost bar leaps
a paper-rush swell as the public comment floods in
drop back to wind for the deadline and the button

divergence_note: The last two dispatches were 'Sorted by a Machine' (top-down-map, scroll-data, data-as-subject, grid-modular, data-brutalist, track-follow, noon-hard, cold institutional grey) and before it the night-water beluga hoax (eye-level-immersive, horizontal-traverse, single-organic-hero, night-practical). This piece shares none of those: it is an orbital-aerial, blueprint-technical shoot over open North Slope tundra under flat overcast light, with an assemble-build motion (a wireframe campus constructing itself) rather than scrolling data or a swimming hero, a rise-reveal camera instead of a track-follow, and a fresh wet-grey tundra and blueprint-cyan palette against the prior grey-fluorescent dossier and teal-black aquarium worlds. The metaphor is new: a proposal drawn as an unbuilt blueprint whose own numbers will not settle, checked by a paper flood of public comment, rather than a machine sorting files or a fake image dissolving. Spatial signature (orbital-aerial, map-territory, assemble-build) does not repeat any of the last four.

taste_dials.style_mode: honest institutional blueprint over real land; sober, not hyped, not doom

references: state DNR lease-parcel plats as a look for the map-territory draw
architectural competition wireframe flythroughs (unbuilt = edges only)
North Slope flat-overcast field photography for the light
